entities.name: Anti-Inflammatory Diet
entities.description: A dietary approach aimed at reducing inflammation in the body.

entities.name: American Heart Association
entities.description: A nonprofit organization focused on heart health and disease prevention.

entities.name: Vitamin C
entities.description: A vitamin that acts as an antioxidant and is important for immune function.

entities.name: Vitamin E
entities.description: A fat-soluble vitamin that acts as an antioxidant and is important for immune function.

entities.name: Selenium
entities.description: A mineral that plays a critical role in metabolism and has antioxidant properties.

entities.name: Zinc
entities.description: A mineral essential for immune function and has antioxidant properties.

entities.name: Copper
entities.description: A mineral that is important for iron metabolism and has antioxidant properties.

entities.name: Manganese
entities.description: A trace mineral that plays a role in bone formation and metabolism.

entities.name: Curcumin
entities.description: A compound found in turmeric known for its anti-inflammatory properties.

entities.name: Green Tea
entities.description: A beverage rich in antioxidants, particularly catechins, which may reduce inflammation.

entities.name: Olive Oil
entities.description: A healthy fat rich in monounsaturated fats and antioxidants, beneficial for heart health.

entities.name: Antioxidants
entities.description: Compounds that inhibit oxidation and neutralize free radicals, potentially reducing inflammation.

relationships.description: The anti-inflammatory diet includes foods rich in Vitamin C to help reduce inflammation.
The anti-inflammatory diet includes foods rich in Vitamin E to help reduce inflammation.
The anti-inflammatory diet includes foods rich in Selenium to help reduce inflammation.
The anti-inflammatory diet includes foods rich in Zinc to help reduce inflammation.
The anti-inflammatory diet includes foods rich in Copper to help reduce inflammation.
The anti-inflammatory diet includes foods rich in Manganese to help reduce inflammation.
The anti-inflammatory diet includes foods containing Curcumin to help reduce inflammation.
The anti-inflammatory diet includes Green Tea, which is rich in antioxidants that may reduce inflammation.
The anti-inflammatory diet recommends Olive Oil as a healthy fat that may help reduce inflammation.
The anti-inflammatory diet is associated with the intake of antioxidants to combat inflammation.
The American Heart Association recommends an anti-inflammatory diet to improve heart health.

entities.name: Anti-Inflammatory Diet
entities.description: A plant-based dietary approach designed to decrease inflammation by limiting added sugar, processed foods, and certain cooking methods, while emphasizing whole plant foods, omega-3 fats, and specific phytonutrients.

entities.name: inflammation
entities.description: A biological response of body tissues to harmful stimuli, such as pathogens, damaged cells, or irritants, which the anti-inflammatory diet aims to reduce.

entities.name: antioxidants
entities.description: Compounds that inhibit oxidation, a chemical reaction that can produce free radicals and chain reactions that may damage cells. They are provided by whole plant foods and help prevent or reduce inflammation.

entities.name: omega-3 fats
entities.description: A type of polyunsaturated fatty acid known for its anti-inflammatory properties, found in foods like anchovies, salmon, walnuts, and flax seeds.

entities.name: American Heart Association (AHA)
entities.description: A national voluntary health organization in the United States that funds cardiovascular medical research, educates consumers on healthy living, and fosters appropriate cardiac care.

entities.name: omega-6 fatty acids
entities.description: A type of polyunsaturated fatty acid, which, when consumed in excess relative to omega-3s, can contribute to inflammatory processes.

entities.name: vitamins
entities.description: Organic compounds that are essential for normal growth and nutrition and are required in small quantities in the diet because they cannot be synthesized by the body.

entities.name: minerals
entities.description: Inorganic elements that are essential nutrients required for various physiological functions in the body.

entities.name: phytonutrients
entities.description: Chemical compounds found in plants that give them their color, odor, and flavor, and show promise in decreasing inflammation and preventing disease.

entities.name: disease
entities.description: A disorder of structure or function in a human, animal, or plant, especially one that produces specific symptoms or that affects a specific location and is not simply a direct result of physical injury.

entities.name: Vitamin C
entities.description: An essential vitamin and antioxidant found in fruits and vegetables, important for immune function and collagen synthesis.

entities.name: Vitamin E
entities.description: A fat-soluble vitamin and antioxidant found in nuts, seeds, and certain vegetables, important for cell protection.

entities.name: Selenium
entities.description: An essential trace mineral with antioxidant properties, found in seafood, animal proteins, and whole grains.

entities.name: Zinc
entities.description: An essential trace mineral vital for immune function, wound healing, and DNA synthesis, found in animal foods and plant foods like seeds and lentils.

entities.name: Copper
entities.description: An essential trace mineral involved in energy production, iron metabolism, and antioxidant defense, found in nuts, seeds, and beans.

entities.name: Manganese
entities.description: An essential trace mineral involved in bone formation, metabolism, and antioxidant systems, found in whole grains, beans, and spinach.

entities.name: Anthocyanins
entities.description: Phytonutrients that give red, blue, and purple plants their color, known for their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects.

entities.name: Ellagic acid
entities.description: A phytonutrient found in pomegranates, walnuts, and green tea, with antioxidant properties.

entities.name: Lycopene
entities.description: A carotenoid phytonutrient that gives red and pink fruits and vegetables their color, known for its antioxidant properties.

entities.name: Carotenoids
entities.description: A class of phytonutrients, including beta-carotene, lutein, and zeaxanthin, that provide yellow and orange colors to plants and act as antioxidants.

entities.name: beta-carotene
entities.description: A specific carotenoid phytonutrient that the body converts to Vitamin A, found in carrots, pumpkin, and sweet potato.

entities.name: Flavonoids
entities.description: A diverse group of phytonutrients, also called polyphenols, found in various plant foods, known for their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory benefits.

entities.name: polyphenols
entities.description: A broad category of phytonutrients, including flavonoids, with antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, found in many plant-based foods.

entities.name: curcumin
entities.description: A polyphenol found in turmeric, known for its potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects.

entities.name: naringenin
entities.description: A flavonoid found in citrus fruit, with antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties.

entities.name: lutein
entities.description: A carotenoid phytonutrient found in corn, orange pepper, and green leafy vegetables, important for eye health.

entities.name: zeaxanthin
entities.description: A carotenoid phytonutrient often found with lutein, important for eye health, present in corn and orange pepper.

entities.name: Glucosinolates
entities.description: Phytonutrients found in cruciferous vegetables like broccoli and cabbage, which can be converted into bioactive compounds like isothiocyanates and sulforaphane.

entities.name: isothiocyanates
entities.description: Bioactive compounds derived from glucosinolates in cruciferous vegetables, known for their anti-inflammatory and anti-cancer properties.

entities.name: sulforaphane
entities.description: A specific isothiocyanate found in cruciferous vegetables, particularly broccoli sprouts, with strong antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects.

entities.name: indole-3-carbinol
entities.description: A compound formed from glucosinolates in cruciferous vegetables, studied for its potential anti-cancer properties.

entities.name: Chlorophyll
entities.description: The green pigment found in plants, involved in photosynthesis, and present in all green vegetables and herbs.

entities.name: Isoflavones
entities.description: A class of phytonutrients, including daidzein and genistein, found in soybeans and soy products, with estrogen-like activity.

entities.name: daidzein
entities.description: A specific isoflavone found in soybeans, with potential health benefits.

entities.name: genistein
entities.description: A specific isoflavone found in soybeans, studied for its antioxidant and anti-cancer properties.

entities.name: Phytosterols
entities.description: Plant sterols structurally similar to cholesterol, found in olive oil and other plant foods, known to help lower cholesterol levels.

entities.name: Catechins
entities.description: A type of flavonoid, such as EGCG, found in green tea, known for its antioxidant properties.

entities.name: EGCG
entities.description: Epigallocatechin gallate, a specific catechin found abundantly in green tea, known for its strong antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects.

entities.name: Resveratrol
entities.description: A polyphenol found in grapes, red wine, dark chocolate, and blueberries, known for its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties.

entities.name: Allicin
entities.description: A sulfur-containing compound found in garlic, onions, and leeks, responsible for their pungent odor and believed to have various health benefits.

entities.name: Lignans
entities.description: A type of polyphenol found in ground flax seeds, other seeds, and nuts, with antioxidant and phytoestrogenic properties.

entities.name: Chlorogenic acid
entities.description: A polyphenol found in coffee and tea, known for its antioxidant activity.

entities.name: antioxidant supplements
entities.description: Dietary supplements containing antioxidants like beta-carotene or vitamin E, which may interact with medicines and should be discussed with a healthcare provider.

entities.name: The Ohio State University Wexner Medical Center
entities.description: A medical institution providing patient care, research, and education, responsible for the patient education material.

entities.name: Library for Health Information
entities.description: A resource within The Ohio State University Wexner Medical Center for obtaining health information.

unique_entity_names: The Ohio State University Wexner Medical Center

langextract_only: the ohio state university wexner medical center
